decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is the primary lever for lateral heat spreading and has not yet been maximized. We will apply a conservative 1.5x increase to the spreader conductivity to improve heat extraction without hitting the upper bound immediately.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal resistance between the hot chip and the cooler boundaries, directly lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate conductivity
Further iterations may require geometric changes (spreader width/height) if material tuning alone is insufficient
